name: Castuera — circular by the Mirador and Campo de Concentración de Castuera
name_original: Castuera — circular por el Mirador y Campo de Concentración de Castuera
region_intro: Hiking around Castuera offers access to expansive natural landscapes within Extremadura, Spain. The region is characterized by its proximity to the La Serena Dam and the Zújar River, forming part of the La Siberia Biosphere Reserve. Terrain includes diverse paths, from sandy terraces to rocky sections, and features historical sites such as castles and hermitages integrated into the natural surroundings. This area provides varied experiences for nature enthusiasts. Best hiking trails around Castuera The most popular hiking route is Camino de Piedraescrita al Río Guadalefra — round trip from Campanario, a 6.7 miles (10.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 55 minutes to complete. This route explores the landscape leading towards the Guadalefra River. Another top favourite among local hikers is Benquerencia Castle Trail, an easy 3.5 miles (5.7 km) path. This trail leads to the historic Benquerencia Castle, offering views of the surrounding valley. Local hikers also love the Cerro de San Cristóbal and La Charca Trail, a 6.3 miles (10.1 km) trail leading through the area around Cerro de San Cristóbal and La Charca, often completed in about 2 hours 42 minutes. Hiking around Castuera is defined by the La Serena Dam reservoir, the La Siberia Biosphere Reserve, and historical castles. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 20 hikers have used komoot to explore Castuera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Castuera?
A: The hiking trails around Castuera offer diverse terrain, ranging from sandy terraces to rocky sections. The region is characterized by its proximity to the La Serena Dam and the Zújar River, forming part of the La Siberia Biosphere Reserve. You'll find paths that traverse expansive natural landscapes, often with historical sites integrated into the surroundings.

Q: Are there easy hiking options in Castuera?
A: Yes, Castuera offers several easy hiking options. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1293234766/benquerencia-castle-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benquerencia Castle Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.5-mile (5.7 km) path that leads to the historic Castle of Benquerencia de la Serena, offering picturesque views of the valley.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in Castuera?
A: There are over 15 hiking routes available around Castuera, catering to various skill levels from easy walks to more challenging routes. These trails allow you to explore the region's natural beauty and historical sites.

Q: What historical sites or natural landmarks can I see while hiking near Castuera?
A: While hiking near Castuera, you can encounter several historical sites and natural landmarks. The region is home to impressive structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6085543" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Benquerencia de la Serena&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5422272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Serena Dam&lt;/a&gt;, one of Europe's largest. You might also come across the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6088024" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Columns of Zalamea de la Serena&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5673791" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parish Church of Esparragosa de Lares&lt;/a&gt;, offering cultural interest amidst the natural surroundings.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes in Castuera?
A: Yes, there are circular hiking routes available. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1293588024/camino-de-piedraescrita-al-rio-guadalefra-round-trip-from-campanario" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Piedraescrita al Río Guadalefra — round trip from Campanario&lt;/a&gt;, a 6.7-mile (10.8 km) trail that explores the landscape towards the Guadalefra River.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Castuera?
A: The best time to hike in Castuera is generally during the spring and autumn months when temperatures are milder. These seasons offer pleasant conditions for exploring the expansive natural landscapes and historical sites without the intense heat of summer or the cooler, potentially wetter days of winter.

Q: What wildlife might I encounter on hikes in Castuera?
A: The region around Castuera, particularly within the La Siberia Biosphere Reserve and near the La Serena Dam, is a Special Protection Area for Birds (SPA). Hikers can expect excellent opportunities for birdwatching, including various aquatic birds and birds of prey.

Q: Are there more challenging routes available in Castuera?
A: For those seeking a greater challenge, Castuera does offer more demanding routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1293744880/valle-del-aliso-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle del Aliso Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is a difficult 11.6-mile (18.7 km) hike with significant elevation changes, providing a more strenuous experience.

Q: How long do hikes around Castuera typically take?
A: Hikes around Castuera vary in length and duration. Easy trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1293234766/benquerencia-castle-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benquerencia Castle Trail&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in under 2 hours, while moderate routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1293855762/cerro-de-san-cristobal-and-la-charca-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de San Cristóbal and La Charca Trail&lt;/a&gt; typically take around 2 hours 42 minutes. Longer, more difficult routes can extend to several hours.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking trails in Castuera?
A: Yes, many of the easier trails around Castuera are suitable for families. Routes that are shorter in distance and have less elevation gain, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1293234766/benquerencia-castle-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benquerencia Castle Trail&lt;/a&gt;, are good options for families looking to enjoy the outdoors together.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Castuera?
A: More than 20 hikers have used komoot to explore Castuera's varied terrain. They often highlight the region's unique blend of natural beauty, such as the La Serena Dam reservoir, and the historical significance of its castles, making for a rewarding hiking experience.

Q: Can I bring my dog on hikes in Castuera?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on trails in Castuera are not detailed, generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking paths in rural Spain, especially if kept on a leash. It's always advisable to check local signage or park regulations for any specific restrictions before heading out with your pet.

Q: Are there any archaeological sites to explore on foot?
A: Yes, the region's history, including Roman mining, hints at varied geological formations and historical interest. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1293874037/cancho-roano-archaeological-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cancho Roano Archaeological Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate 7.5-mile (12 km) route through an area with historical significance.
tips: Explore the history and beauty of Castuera, a town with deep roots and an impressive natural environment. 🌿🏰

A picturesque place on the high point overlooking the town of Castuera. From the exposed rock outcrop, at the north-west end of the Sierra de Castuera, there are incredible views of the surrounding landscape.

A historic site with a dark past. The location of a Franco-era civil war concentration camp. To this day, the sacred ground surrounding this site is haunted by the memories of the families of the lost.
